skill: Identify the first phoneme in a spoken word.
notes: Audio in / audio out. No letters on screen.

skill: Identify the last phoneme in a spoken word.
Identify the middle vowel phoneme in a CVC word.
Blend 2–3 spoken phonemes into a word.
notes: Currently the only PA node implemented in src/data/skill_nodes.json.

skill: Blend 4–5 spoken phonemes into a word with blends, digraphs, or final clusters.
Segment a spoken CVC word into its 3 phonemes.
notes: Per-phoneme scoring requires Azure Pronunciation Assessment. Current MVP swaps to PA_04 blend until ASR lands.

skill: Segment a spoken word with blends or final clusters into 4–5 phonemes.
Say a word with the initial phoneme removed.
Say a word with the final phoneme removed.
Substitute a phoneme to make a new word.
notes: Strongest predictor of decoding skill among PA tasks per Kilpatrick (2015).

skill: Produce the sound for each of m, s, t, p, n, c, f, h shown in isolation.
Produce the sound for b, d, g, j, k, l, r, v, w, y, z, qu, x.
Produce the short sounds /a/ and /i/.
Produce the short sounds /o/, /u/, /e/.
Decode and spell CVC words with short a.
Decode and spell CVC words with short i.
Decode and spell CVC words with short o.
Decode and spell CVC words with short u.
Decode and spell CVC words with short e.
Recognize and spell 6 anchor HFW that unlock earliest connected text.
examples: the, a, I, is, was, to
notes: Split from original HFW_01_set1 per Research Review v1.0. Enables FL_01 cold-passage reading earlier in the chain.

skill: Recognize and spell the remaining 19 phase-1 high-frequency words.
examples: do, of, you, said, my, by, has, have, he, she, we, me, be, are, for, with, this, that, from

skill: Read a cold passage of short-vowel CVC words + anchor HFW at ≥30 WCPM with ≥95% accuracy.
examples: Sam has a pet cat. The cat sits on the mat. Sam pets it.
notes: Fluency gate. Blocks all downstream connected-text work. trickle_down enabled per Research Review v1.0 — cumulative passage success is implicit review for every node it draws on.

skill: Decode and spell words with initial s-blends.
Decode and spell words with initial l-blends.
Decode and spell words with initial r-blends.

topic: 2.3.1. Final Blends (nd, nt, nk, mp, st, ft, lt, sk, sp)
skill: Decode and spell words with final blends.
Decode and spell words with the digraph sh (initial and final).
Decode and spell words with the digraph ch (initial and final).
Decode and spell words with th (voiced and unvoiced).
notes: Voiced /ð/ (that, with) and unvoiced /θ/ (thin, thick) are different phonemes mapping to the same digraph. Set-for-variability prompt required: assessment must include items for both values.

skill: Decode and spell words ending in -ck after a short vowel.
notes: Phonologically a digraph; pedagogically attaches to short-vowel CVC. Sits parallel to DG_sh/ch/th, not strictly serial.

skill: Decode and spell words ending in -ng and -nk.
Decode and spell words with -tch and -dge after a short vowel.
Recognize and spell 25 phase-2 HFW.
examples: they, were, your, what, when, where, who, there, here, would, could, should, one, two, some, come, put, saw, want, going, like, make, made

skill: Decode and spell plural and 3rd-person endings -s, -es.
Read a cold passage with blends, digraphs, -ck/-ng/-nk, HFW 1–2 at ≥50 WCPM with ≥96% accuracy.
examples: Passage uses words like 'stop, ship, lunch, pink, with, said'
notes: Fluency gate. trickle_down enabled per Research Review v1.0.

skill: Decode and spell words with the a_e long-a pattern, contrasting with CVC.
Decode and spell words with the i_e long-i pattern.
Decode and spell words with the o_e long-o pattern.
Decode and spell words with the u_e pattern (both /yoo/ and /oo/ values).
Decode and spell -ed and -ing on base words (no doubling/dropping yet).
Read a cold passage with silent-e patterns + earlier mastered material at ≥60 WCPM, ≥96% accuracy.
examples: Passage uses 'made, time, home, like, cake'
notes: Fluency gate. trickle_down enabled per Research Review v1.0.

skill: Decode and spell long-a vowel teams ai (medial) and ay (final).
Decode and spell long-e vowel teams ee and ea.
Decode and spell long-o vowel teams oa and ow.
Decode and spell long-i patterns igh and ie.
Decode and spell oo with both values: /oo/ as in moon and /ʊ/ as in book.
notes: Set-for-variability prompt: try both sounds.

skill: Decode and spell the diphthong /ou/ spelled ou and ow.
Decode and spell the diphthong /oi/ spelled oi (medial) and oy (final).
Decode and spell words with r-controlled ar.
Decode and spell words with r-controlled or.
Decode and spell the /er/ sound spelled er, ir, and ur.
notes: Spelling choice ambiguity expected — multiple correct spellings flagged in encoding task.

skill: Decode and spell words with soft c (/s/ before e, i, y) and soft g (/j/ before e, i, y).
Decode and spell compound words built from previously mastered patterns.
notes: Bridge into P2 Multisyllabic strand.

skill: Decode and spell two-syllable words with closed syllables (VC/CV split).
Read a cold connected passage spanning all mastered patterns at end-of-G2 fluency benchmark.
examples: Passage uses r-controlled, vowel teams, 2-syllable words, HFW 1–2.
notes: Terminal fluency gate for K–2 phase. Closes the course. trickle_down enabled per Research Review v1.0.

skill: Infer the meaning of a tier-2 academic word from the sentence around it.
examples: The dog was reluctant to go in the water — he didn't want to get wet.
notes: Phase A1. Tap-to-pick MC; no ASR.

skill: Answer a literal-recall question by locating the answer in a short passage.
examples: Maya rode her bike to the park. She brought her red kite. → What did Maya bring?
notes: Phase A2. Answer is lifted directly from the passage.

skill: Answer an inference question — the answer is implied, not stated directly.
examples: Sam put on his coat and grabbed an umbrella. → What is the weather like?
notes: Phase A3. Answer requires bridging from text + prior knowledge.

skill: Apply a short content reading to answer a knowledge question.
examples: Bees make honey from flower nectar. → Where does honey come from?
notes: Phase A4. Short content reading + recall. Builds knowledge alongside reading.

skill: Identify the main idea — what the whole passage is mostly about.
examples: Bees buzz from flower to flower collecting nectar. They take it back to the hive. There they turn it into honey. — Main idea: How bees make honey.
notes: Phase B2. Gist-level synthesis of a short passage.

skill: Identify the theme (life lesson or message) of a short story.
examples: The rabbit ran ahead and napped, sure he'd win. The slow tortoise kept going and crossed the line first. — Theme: Steady effort beats hurry.
notes: Phase B2. Theme is broader than main idea — a takeaway, not just a topic.

skill: Choose the best one-sentence summary of a short passage.
examples: [passage about a school field trip] → Summary: The class visited the zoo and learned about animals.
notes: Phase B2. Distinguish best summary from too-detailed and too-narrow distractors.

skill: Identify cause-effect relationships in a passage.
examples: It rained all night, so the field was muddy in the morning. — Cause: rain. Effect: muddy field.
notes: Phase B2. Signal words: because, so, therefore, since.

skill: Identify how two things in a passage are similar and different.
examples: Frogs and toads both have four legs, but frogs have smooth skin and toads have bumpy skin.
notes: Phase B2. Signal words: both, like, unlike, but, however.

skill: Identify the order in which events happen in a passage.
examples: First Ana mixed the flour and sugar. Then she added eggs. Finally she put the batter in the oven.
notes: Phase B2. Signal words: first, next, then, after, finally.

skill: Identify the problem in a passage and the solution the characters try.
examples: The ducks could not cross the busy road. A kind officer stopped traffic so the ducks could waddle across safely.
notes: Phase B2. Common narrative structure across G3-5.

skill: Identify whether the author's purpose is to inform, entertain, or persuade.
examples: 'Recycling helps save trees and energy. You should recycle every day!' — Purpose: to persuade.
notes: Phase B2. PIE: Persuade / Inform / Entertain.

skill: Infer why a character does what they do in a story.
examples: Lin checked the clock and ran out the door without breakfast. → Motivation: she was late for school.
notes: Phase B2. Reading character behavior + emotions.

skill: Identify the specific sentence in a passage that best supports a given claim.
examples: Claim: Otters are good swimmers. Best support: 'Otters can swim faster than most fish in the river.'
notes: Phase B2. Text-evidence skill central to G3-5 standards.

skill: Identify the meaning of similes, metaphors, and common idioms in context.
examples: 'It's raining cats and dogs.' → It is raining very hard.
notes: Phase B2. Non-literal language; key for G3+ comprehension.

skill: Combine information from two short passages to answer a question.
examples: Passage A: penguins live in cold places. Passage B: penguins eat fish. → Where do animals that eat fish in cold places live?
notes: Phase B2. Hardest comprehension node — requires holding two sources in mind.

skill: Build cumulative knowledge about the water cycle: evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection.
examples: Connected passages on the sun, evaporation, clouds, rain, rivers, oceans — concepts compound across the arc.
notes: Phase B3 arc 1. Each passage introduces vocab (evaporate, condense, precipitation, vapor) that later passages reuse. Final item synthesizes across the arc.

skill: Build cumulative knowledge about the solar system: sun, planets, orbits, gravity, asteroids.
examples: Connected passages on the sun, planet order, gravity, day/night, moons — concepts compound.
notes: Phase B3 arc 2. Vocab: orbit, gravity, rotation, planet, asteroid, moon.

module: 6.3. Biology: How the Body Fights Germs
skill: Build cumulative knowledge about how the body defends against germs.
examples: Connected passages on germs, skin, white blood cells, vaccines, fever — concepts compound.
notes: Phase B3 arc 3. Vocab: germ, virus, bacteria, immune, vaccine, antibody.

skill: Build cumulative knowledge about Ancient Egypt — the Nile, pharaohs, pyramids, hieroglyphs.
examples: Connected passages on the Nile, farming, pharaohs, pyramids, gods, hieroglyphs — concepts compound.
notes: Phase B3 arc 4. Vocab: Nile, pharaoh, pyramid, hieroglyph, flood, civilization. The agent's anchor example.

skill: Read a cold connected passage at end-of-G3 benchmark fluency.
examples: 150-word cold passage spanning G3 vocabulary and morphology.
notes: Phase B4. Per agent: G3 fluency benchmark is 110 WCPM at ≥97% accuracy on a 150-word cold passage. Closes G3 phonics+fluency.

skill: Read a cold connected passage at end-of-G4 benchmark fluency.
examples: 175-word cold passage spanning G4 vocabulary, morphology, and multisyllabic patterns.
notes: Phase B4. G4 fluency benchmark per agent: 125 WCPM at ≥97% accuracy.

skill: Read a cold connected passage at end-of-G5 benchmark fluency.
examples: 200-word cold passage spanning G5 vocabulary, morphology, and content-area text.
notes: Phase B4. Terminal G5 fluency gate per agent: 140 WCPM at ≥97% accuracy. Closes the K-5 fluency arc.

skill: Decode and define words using the prefix un-.
notes: Phase B1. Most common English prefix. Negation/reversal.

skill: Decode and define words using the prefix re-.
Decode and define words using the prefix pre-.
Decode and define words using the prefix dis-.
Decode and define words using the prefix mis-.
Decode and define words using the prefix non-.
Decode and define words using the prefixes in- and im- (negation).
notes: im- before m/p/b; in- elsewhere.

skill: Decode and define words using the prefix sub-.
Decode and define words using the prefix super-.
Decode and define words using the prefix inter-.
Decode and define words using the prefix trans-.
Decode and define words using the prefix tele-.
notes: Anchor example from agent: 'After tele- is taught, student decomposes telegraph, telepathy, televise.'

skill: Decode and define words using the prefix anti-.
Decode and define words using the prefix mid-.
Decode and define words using the prefix over-.
Decode and define words using the prefix under-.
Decode and define words using the prefix fore-.
Decode and define words using the prefix ex-.
Decode and define words using the agent suffix -er.
notes: Agent (person who does X). Compare with comparative -er and noun -er.

skill: Decode and define comparative adjectives ending in -er.
Decode and define superlative adjectives ending in -est.
Decode and define adverbs ending in -ly.
Decode and define adjectives ending in -ful.
Decode and define adjectives ending in -less.
Decode and define adjectives ending in -able.
Decode and define nouns ending in -ness.
Decode and define nouns ending in -ment.
Decode and define nouns ending in -tion.
Decode and define adjectives ending in -ous.
Decode and define adjectives ending in -ive.
Decode and define words using the root port.
Decode and define words using the root struct.
Decode and define words using the root dict.
Decode and define words using the root ject.
Decode and define words using the root scrib/script.
Decode and define words using the root vis/vid.
Decode and define words using the root aud.
Decode and define words using the root graph.
Decode and define words using the root phon.
Decode and define words using the root geo.
Decode and define words using the root bio.
Decode and define words using the root hydro.
Decode and define words using the root therm.
Decode and define words using the root meter.
Decode and define words using the root astro.
Decode and define words using the root act.
Break a word into a prefix and a base word.
Break a word into a base word and a suffix.
Break a longer word into a prefix, a base, and a suffix.
Break an unfamiliar word into its parts using known roots and affixes.

topic: 6.5.1. How Energy Moves Through an Ecosystem
skill: Build cumulative knowledge about food chains: producers, consumers, predators, decomposers.
examples: Connected passages on the sun, plants, herbivores, predators, decomposers — concepts compound.
notes: Phase C arc 5. Vocab: producer, consumer, herbivore, carnivore, predator, prey, decomposer.

skill: Build cumulative knowledge about simple machines: lever, ramp, wheel and axle, pulley.
examples: Connected passages on work, levers, inclined planes, wheels, pulleys — concepts compound.
notes: Phase C arc 6. Vocab: simple machine, work, force, lever, fulcrum, inclined plane, pulley.

skill: Build cumulative knowledge about the American Revolution: colonies, taxes, independence.
examples: Connected passages on the colonies, taxes, the Declaration of Independence, the war — concepts compound.
notes: Phase C arc 7. Vocab: colony, colonist, tax, representation, independence, declaration.
